title: odium: export Grafana/Prometheus/Loki creds to EC2 env for metrics/log push
description: In aztec10 run (2026-04-17), odium did not export Grafana Cloud push credentials to the EC2 instance, so the ODM bootstrap couldn't push metrics/logs. See geo memory/reference_grafana.md for endpoints + user IDs. Odium should read these (from ~/.odium/env or similar) and pass through to terraform apply / user-data env so prometheus+loki agents run on the instance.

title: odium: ec2_destroy falsely reports success after SNS-principal terraform error; SSH shutdown is not termination
description:
During aztec10 cleanup (2026-04-17), odium's ec2_destroy hit a Terraform 'contains invalid principals' error on the SNS topic refresh. Instead of surfacing the failure, odium:
1. Attempted an SSH workaround via 'sudo shutdown -h now' — which halts but does not terminate the instance (EBS keeps billing, state stays dirty).
2. Reported instance_ip='DESTROYED' in state despite the instance still being present (stopped, not terminated).

User had to run 'terraform destroy -refresh=false' manually to complete the cleanup.

Fix needed:
1. ec2_destroy handler: if stderr contains 'contains invalid principals', retry once with -refresh=false before giving up.
2. After any claimed-successful destroy, verify via AWS describe-instances (filters: tag:Purpose=odm-run, state=running|stopped|pending) — if anything remains, downgrade status to 'partial'.
3. System prompt: never fall back to SSH shutdown as a substitute for terraform destroy. If destroy fails, STOP and report.

title: odium: pass odm_image var to infra/ec2/ terraform apply
description: During aztec10 validation run (2026-04-17), odium launched ODM without overriding the odm_image terraform variable. Default (opendronemap/odm:3.5.6) was used instead of the caller-requested 658302145097.dkr.ecr.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/odm:v3.6.0-baseline. Run had to be aborted ~45 min in. Odium must accept an image override (either from user prompt, .env, or explicit arg) and pass it as -var="odm_image=..." when running terraform apply.

title: odium: read surveyor email from .env / config (not placeholder)
description: In aztec10 run (2026-04-17) odium used placeholder 'surveyor@example.com' for SNS notify_email instead of asking or reading from config. User had to correct mid-run. Odium should: (a) read ~/.odium/env or similar, (b) fall back to prompting, (c) never default to a placeholder that gets passed to SNS.

title: odium: list_odm_images tool to let user pick image from ECR + Docker Hub + github release
description:
Add a list_odm_images tool that enumerates available ODM Docker images from two sources and presents them to the user to choose from at launch time. Motivation: users shouldn't have to memorize image URIs or know about ECR vs Docker Hub; the agent should present options with enough annotation (size, pushed date, stable vs rolling) to pick confidently.

Sources:
- ECR (their custom builds): aws ecr describe-images --repository-name odm
- Docker Hub (official upstream): GET hub.docker.com/v2/repositories/opendronemap/odm/tags/
- GitHub (latest stable release): gh release view --repo OpenDroneMap/ODM

Behavior:
- If ODM_IMAGE_DEFAULT env is set, use it silently — no prompt.
- If unset AND user wants to launch, call list_odm_images, present list, ask which.
- If user says 'pick', 'list', 'which images' → always call.

Annotate Docker Hub's 'latest' tag as rolling/master, NOT latest stable — haiku previously conflated these (invented 'opendronemap/odm:3.6.0' which does not exist on Docker Hub).

title: odium: end-user pipeline orchestrator (Claude Agent SDK + GUI)
description:
Build a standalone agent that walks a working drone surveyor through the full geo pipeline end-to-end. Claude Agent SDK + Flask GUI. ODM-only MVP first, Pix4D later.

Design doc: docs/plans/geo-agent.md

Migrated from geo-nha8.

title: Per-job budget limit for EC2 runs
description: Add a per-job budget limit to prevent runaway EC2 costs. The state already tracks estimated_cost and launch_time, and the instance reports INSTANCE_COST_PER_HOUR. Use these to: (1) let the user set a max_budget in ec2_launch or state metadata, (2) have ec2_status calculate elapsed cost = hours × hourly rate, (3) warn when approaching the limit, (4) optionally auto-stop the instance at the cap. Consider: where does the check run — odium polling, or a cron on the instance itself (more reliable if odium isn't running)? The spot-watcher.sh or odm-progress.sh on the instance could be extended to self-terminate at the budget cap.
close_reason: Moving to geo repo — budget enforcement belongs in infra/ec2 (instance-side), not the orchestrator

title: Data lifecycle management (local/NAS/S3 inventory, archiving, cost tracking)
description:
Track which jobs have how much data where (local SSD, NAS, S3), related AWS costs per job, and assist with archiving/cleanup.

- Job inventory: name, location(s), size, status, last accessed
- S3 cost tracking per job (tied to job tag)
- NAS sync status
- Archive workflows: local → NAS, S3 cleanup after download
- "What's using space?" and "What can I clean up?" queries

Deferred to future session — design doc reference: docs/plans/odium.md (multi-job awareness section).

title: odium: track workflow-state to avoid suggesting already-completed work
description: In aztec10 (2026-04-17), odium suggested user 'tag the 31 CHK targets in GCPEditorPro while ODM runs' — but aztec_tagged.txt was already fully populated (copied from aztec7, 10 GCPs + 31 CHKs, 161+514 obs all pre-tagged). Odium should check whether tagged.txt is complete before suggesting tagging work. Minimum: inspect whether 'confidence' column is 'tagged' for the expected points; skip suggestion if so.
